purpose: Burn-down classification atop tests/conformance/dlfcn_replace_boundary_sentinel.v1.json. The sentinel keeps the eight current host-delegation call sites visible; this file records, per call site, whether it is a standalone replacement blocker (must be removed before current_level can advance to L2/L3) or whether it is structurally below the hardened interpose path (only reachable from a runtime path that hardened interpose turns off). The harness gate enforces the classification per the source-code guards and refuses any silent reclassification that would lift standalone replacement blockers without a native replacement.

classifications.rationale: Guarded by `runtime_policy::bootstrap_passthrough_active()` (dlfcn_abi.rs:216); bootstrap pass-through runs ONLY before runtime mode resolution, which itself happens before any L1 path can be taken. L1 promotion implies the runtime is past phase-0 startup, so this branch is unreachable at L1 by construction.

Same `bootstrap_passthrough_active()` gate at dlfcn_abi.rs:609 (dlclose). Closes host-loaded handles created during bootstrap; if dlopen_bootstrap_passthrough is unreachable at L1, dlclose_bootstrap_passthrough has no live host handles to close.
RTLD_NEXT walk via host_dlvsym_next_raw is the only versioned-symbol resolution path; L1 must replace this with native version-chain enumeration.
Helper used by host-handle dlsym delegation; L1 must replace with native dlsym backed by the loader's own symbol table.
Helper used by host-handle dlvsym delegation; L1 must replace with native dlvsym.
Post-bootstrap dlopen path delegates to host loader because we have no native ELF loader yet; standalone replacement promotion requires a native loader, so this site is the central blocker.
Host handles obtained via host dlopen need host dlsym to resolve symbols; L1 native dlopen would obviate this site by producing native handles.
Companion of dlsym_post_bootstrap_host_handle; native handles will not need host dlclose at L1.
